title: 9 BPRP Settlement and Tracing
body_text:
9	BPRP Settlement and Tracing
This Chapter applies to BC and Ontario credit unions and other Central 1 clients. Financial institutions in other provinces, contact your provincial central for information on BPRP settlement and tracing.
9.1	BPRP Settlement
Settlement between Central 1 and the Corporate Creditor
Central 1 credits the Corporate CreditorГўВҖВҷs account with your financial institution via Direct Deposit. Settlement is provided on business days only. Payments that are received at Central 1 on Fridays, Saturdays, or Sundays are posted as one credit to the Corporate CreditorГўВҖВҷs account on the following business day.
Settlement between the Corporate Creditor and its Customers
The Corporate Creditor must agree to deem its customer to have paid the Remittance amount on the Payment Date, as per CPA Rule H6, Rules Pertaining to the Inter Financial Institution Exchange of Bill Payment Remittances for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement.
Payments are listed on the ERTV report. The Corporate Creditor uses the ERTV report to manually credit customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. See SectionВ 10.4, ERTV ГўВҖВ“ Tracing Vendor Report.
If a data file was requested, the Corporate Creditor uses the data file to automatically credit customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. See Chapter 11, BPRP Data Files.
Settlement between Central 1 and your Financial Institution
Central 1 posts the monthly BPRP fees to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs current account on the second business day of the month, effective the last business day of the previous month. The debits are displayed on your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs monthly statement using the code ГўВҖВңERГўВҖВқ. A separate entry is posted per Corporate Creditor (vendor on the ERMC report). An electronic copy of the ERMC report, is uploaded to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs FTP folder. See Section 10.3, ERMC ГўВҖВ“ Bill Payment Remittance Processing Monthly Charge Report.
9.2	Tracing Payments to Corporate Creditors (Electronic Remittances)
Financial institutions enrolled in Central 1ГўВҖВҷs BPRP initiate trace requests for their own Corporate CreditorsГўВҖВҷ electronic payments via CentralВ 1ГўВҖВҷs Online Tracing System (OLT). This only applies to payments originating from Central 1. See the Online Tracing System Part for procedures. Central 1 will issue an adjustment if necessary.
For financial institutions with clients enrolled in BPRP services, they must directly contact the Direct Clearer for any trace and/or return requests. See the BPRP Traces or Returns Contact List, Form 10015, for more information. This form provides instructions on how to determine the originating institution and the contact information of the specific Direct Clearer who processed the payment.
Note:	CPA Rule E3, Rules Applicable to Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Transactions, stipulates that Central 1 is not obligated to respond to trace requests for payments that are less than $20 or that are received more than 12 months after the Value Date of the Item. Refer to the Payments Canada website for more information.
Related Information
BPRP Traces or Returns Contact List,
Form 10015
9.3	Tracing Payments to Corporate Creditors (Paper Remittances)
Central 1 does not accept paper bill payment remittances.
If the Corporate Creditor requires assistance with a trace request, it must contact its financial institution. If the financial institution is unable to resolve the trace, it can contact Central 1 as follows:
Central 1 Credit Union
Remittance Processing
Phone:
604-730-6379
or
1-800-661-6813
, ext.
6379
Fax:
604-714-5860
Note:	CPA Rule E3, Rules Applicable to Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Transactions, stipulates that Central 1 is not obligated to respond to trace requests for payments that are less than $20 or that are received more than 12 months after the Value Date of the Item. Refer to the Payments Canada website for more information.
END
